questions.question: Which standards body leads QKD-specific standardization in Europe?
questions.explanation: ETSI (European Telecommunications Standards Institute) Industry Specification Group on QKD (ISG-QKD) leads QKD standardization in Europe, producing key standards like GS QKD 004 (interface) and GS QKD 018 (orchestration).

questions.question: What does ETSI GS QKD 004 standardize?
questions.explanation: ETSI GS QKD 004 standardizes the Application Interface - how applications request keys from QKD systems. This enables applications to work with different vendors' QKD equipment.

questions.question: Which organization focuses on quantum internet architecture from a research perspective?
questions.explanation: The IETF Quantum Internet Research Group (QIRG) explores architectural principles for the quantum internet. It takes a research-oriented approach to future protocol development.

questions.question: What is a major gap in current quantum network standardization?
questions.explanation: While application interfaces and security requirements are well-standardized, quantum repeater protocols and routing standards are still in research phase. Multi-vendor repeater interoperability is not yet possible.

questions.question: What does interoperability at the physical layer require?

options.text: Standard wavelength plans and timing synchronization

questions.explanation: Physical layer interoperability requires agreement on wavelength assignments (e.g., O-band for quantum), timing synchronization standards, and connector/fiber specifications for different vendors to work together.

questions.question: What is the role of ITU-T in quantum network standards?
questions.explanation: ITU-T focuses on integrating quantum networks with existing telecommunications infrastructure. Standards like X.1710 (security framework) and X.1712 (architecture) address telecom-specific needs.

questions.question: All layers of the quantum network stack are now fully standardized.
questions.explanation: False. While application interfaces and some security requirements are well standardized, network layer (routing), link layer (entanglement protocols), and physical layer standards are still emerging or incomplete.
